coverage.forecastRange: 2026-2028 third-party consensus; FY2026 capex also has official guidance
coverage.availabilityNote: FY2016-FY2025 are consolidated annual actuals. FY2026-FY2028 revenue, net income, PP&E additions and FCF are third-party consensus estimates from an openly accessible MarketScreener page. ExxonMobil's FY2026 official cash-capex guidance is USD27-29bn; its midpoint is retained in the normalized table, while the dashboard uses the consensus series for a consistent forecast presentation. No annual forecasts were retained after FY2028.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income. FY2016 revenue is restated to the FY2017 comparable presentation after the sales-based tax reporting-policy change; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2017 10-K and FY2017 results are the primary comparable sources. ExxonMobil restated FY2016 total revenues and other income to USD208,114m after its FY2017 sales-based tax reporting-policy change; the original FY2016 10-K reported USD218,608m. Net income, cash flow, cash and PP&E cash additions are cross-checked to the original FY2016 filing/results and SEC Companyfacts. FCF is operating cash flow plus investing cash flow, the company definition used in this period.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2017 10-K is the primary source. The official FY2017 results release confirms net income and annual capital and exploration expenditure; SEC Companyfacts cross-checks the audited cash-flow tags. FCF is operating cash flow plus investing cash flow.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2018 10-K is the primary source. The official FY2018 results release confirms net income and operating cash flow; SEC Companyfacts cross-checks audited cash-flow tags. FCF is operating cash flow plus investing cash flow.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2019 10-K is the primary source. The official FY2019 results release confirms net income and operating cash flow; SEC Companyfacts cross-checks audited cash-flow tags. FCF is operating cash flow plus investing cash flow.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2020 10-K is the primary source. The official FY2020 results release confirms the net loss, operating cash flow and annual capital spending; SEC Companyfacts cross-checks audited cash-flow tags. FCF is operating cash flow plus investing cash flow.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2021 10-K is the primary source. ExxonMobil's FY2022 results release provides a formal FY2021 FCF comparison; it agrees with operating cash flow plus investing cash flow.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2022 10-K is the primary source. The official FY2022 results release agrees on net income, operating cash flow, reported FCF and PP&E additions.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2023 10-K is the primary source. FY2024 results restated FY2023 FCF to USD36,219m under the updated company definition, including noncontrolling-interest inflows. The original FY2023 release showed USD36,095m under its then-current definition.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2024 10-K is the primary source. The official FY2024 results release agrees on net income, operating cash flow, reported FCF and PP&E additions; FY2025 results repeats the FY2024 FCF reconciliation.

metrics.basis: Exxon Mobil Corporation consolidated; revenue is total revenues and other income; capex is cash paid for PP&E additions
metrics.verificationNote: FY2025 10-K is the primary source. The official FY2025 results release agrees on net income, operating cash flow, reported FCF, PP&E additions and period-end cash.

metrics.status: Third-party consensus forecast; official cash-capex guidance is USD27-29bn
metrics.basis: MarketScreener analyst consensus in USD million; ExxonMobil official FY2026 cash-capex guidance midpoint is separately recorded as USD28,000m
metrics.verificationNote: Revenue, net income, PP&E additions and FCF are third-party consensus estimates. ExxonMobil's official FY2026 cash-capex range is USD27-29bn, consistent in direction but not identical to the consensus PP&E-additions estimate. Operating cash flow and cash balance are left blank because the source does not provide a directly aligned annual estimate.

metrics.verificationNote: Revenue, net income, PP&E additions and FCF are third-party consensus estimates. Operating cash flow and cash balance are left blank because no directly aligned accessible annual estimate was retained.

profitability.title: Revenue and Net Income
profitability.subtitle: USD million
profitability.basisLabel: Consolidated group actuals FY2016-FY2025; analyst consensus FY2026-FY2028
profitability.note: Forecast values are third-party consensus estimates; actual values are official consolidated disclosures.

cashInvestment.title: Investments and Free Cash Flow
cashInvestment.titleCn: ExxonMobil 投资与自由现金流
cashInvestment.subtitle: USD million
cashInvestment.basisLabel: PP&E additions as investments; company-reported FCF actuals; analyst consensus forecast
cashInvestment.note: Investment bars show cash paid for PP&E additions. Company FCF definition changed in FY2024; the verification sheet records the restatement.

definitions.definition: Total revenues and other income in ExxonMobil's consolidated disclosures. This is the reported top-line basis used by the selected third-party consensus source. FY2016 uses the USD208,114m comparable amount restated in FY2017 following ExxonMobil's sales-based tax reporting-policy change; the original FY2016 disclosure reported USD218,608m.
Net income attributable to ExxonMobil (U.S. GAAP).
Net cash provided by operating activities (U.S. GAAP).

Company-reported non-GAAP FCF where disclosed. For FY2016-FY2020 it is calculated as operating cash flow plus net cash used in investing activities, the company definition used in those periods. ExxonMobil updated the FCF definition in FY2024 to exclude cash acquired from mergers and acquisitions and include inflows from noncontrolling interests for major projects.

Cash paid for additions to property, plant and equipment, shown as a negative outflow in normalized data and positive columns in the dashboard. This is a consistent PP&E cash-capex proxy and excludes additional investments and advances.
Cash and cash equivalents at year end, excluding restricted cash where separately classified.
Net increase or decrease in cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash, including exchange-rate effects, from the audited cash-flow statement.

FY2026-FY2028 revenue, net income, PP&E additions and FCF are third-party consensus estimates. The official FY2026 cash-capex guidance of USD27-29bn is retained separately as a midpoint of USD28,000m.
